from source import filter_expensive_items

def test_filter_expensive_items_with_string_price():
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'C', 'price': 150}
    ]
    threshold = 150
    # The buggy code will raise a TypeError when comparing '200' > 150.
    # The fixed code should handle type coercion and include item B.
    try:
        result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)
    except TypeError:
        assert False, "Function raised TypeError due to string price comparison"
    # Assert that the function returns items with price > 150
    # Item B has string price '200', which should be treated as 200 > 150
    assert len(result) == 1, f"Expected 1 item, got {len(result)}"
    assert result[0]['name'] == 'B', f"Expected item B, got {result[0]['name']}"
